subUnits.title: 7.1 Trigonometric Ratios and the Unit Circle
notes: Trigonometric Ratios and the Unit Circle
- A circle with a radius of 1 unit, centered at the origin (0,0) of a Cartesian plane.
- Any point on the circle has coordinates (x, y) = (cos θ, sin θ).
- Mnemonic: 'All Students Take Calculus' (All, Sin, Tan, Cos are positive in Q1, Q2, Q3, Q4 respectively).
- Understanding the unit circle is foundational for advanced mathematics and physics, which Nigerian students will encounter in SS2 and SS3.
diagramDescriptions: The Unit Circle showing coordinates and quadrant signs
subUnits.youtubeQuery: SS1 Mathematics unit circle trigonometric ratios quadrants site:youtube.com

quiz.q: What is the radius of the unit circle?
quiz.explanation: By definition, the unit circle has a radius of exactly 1 unit.

quiz.q: In the unit circle, the x-coordinate of a point at angle θ represents:
quiz.explanation: The coordinates are (cos θ, sin θ), so x = cos θ.

quiz.q: In Quadrant II (90° to 180°), which trigonometric ratio is positive?
quiz.explanation: In Q2, only Sine is positive (All Students Take Calculus).

quiz.q: In Quadrant III (180° to 270°), which trigonometric ratio is positive?
quiz.explanation: In Q3, only Tangent is positive.

quiz.q: In Quadrant IV (270° to 360°), which trigonometric ratio is positive?
quiz.explanation: In Q4, only Cosine is positive.

quiz.q: What is the value of sin 180°?
quiz.explanation: At 180°, the point on the unit circle is (-1, 0), so sin 180° = y = 0.

quiz.q: What is the value of cos 270°?
quiz.explanation: At 270°, the point is (0, -1), so cos 270° = x = 0.

quiz.q: The mnemonic 'All Students Take Calculus' helps remember:
opts: Which trig ratios are positive in each quadrant
The formula for the unit circle
quiz.explanation: ASTC helps remember that All, Sin, Tan, Cos are positive in Q1, Q2, Q3, Q4 respectively.

quiz.q: If sin θ is negative and cos θ is negative, in which quadrant is θ?
quiz.explanation: Both sin and cos are negative only in Quadrant III.

quiz.q: What is the value of tan 360°?
quiz.explanation: At 360°, the point is (1, 0). tan 360° = sin/cos = 0/1 = 0.

subUnits.title: 7.2 Drawing the Graph of Sine (0° to 360°)
notes: Drawing the Graph of Sine (0° to 360°)
1. Key Points for the Sine Graph:
- The sine graph is a smooth, continuous wave.
- It starts at the origin, goes up to 1 at 90°, crosses the x-axis at 180°, goes down to -1 at 270°, and returns to 0 at 360°.
- Draw the x-axis (angles from 0° to 360°) and y-axis (values from -1 to 1).
- Plot the key points and join them with a smooth curve.
- Wave graphs like the sine wave are fundamental in understanding alternating current (AC) electricity used in the Nigerian power grid.
diagramDescriptions: Graph of y = sin θ from 0° to 360° showing key points
subUnits.youtubeQuery: SS1 Mathematics drawing graph of sine 0 to 360 degrees site:youtube.com

quiz.q: What is the value of sin 0°?
quiz.explanation: The sine graph starts at (0°, 0).

quiz.q: What is the maximum value of the sine graph?
quiz.explanation: The sine wave peaks at y = 1 at 90°.

quiz.q: At what angle does the sine graph cross the x-axis for the first time after 0°?
quiz.explanation: sin 180° = 0, so it crosses the x-axis at 180°.

quiz.q: What is the minimum value of the sine graph?
quiz.explanation: The sine wave reaches its lowest point at y = -1 at 270°.

quiz.q: What is the shape of the sine graph?
quiz.explanation: The sine graph is a smooth, continuous wave.

quiz.q: At 90°, the sine graph is at its:
quiz.explanation: sin 90° = 1, which is the maximum value.

quiz.q: At 270°, the sine graph is at its:
quiz.explanation: sin 270° = -1, which is the minimum value.

quiz.q: What is the value of sin 360°?
quiz.explanation: The sine wave completes one full cycle and returns to 0 at 360°.

quiz.q: When drawing the sine graph, the x-axis represents:
quiz.explanation: The x-axis represents the independent variable, which is the angle θ.

quiz.q: The sine graph is positive in which quadrants?
quiz.explanation: Sine is positive in Quadrant I (0-90) and Quadrant II (90-180).

subUnits.title: 7.3 Drawing the Graph of Cosine and Interpreting Graphs
notes: Drawing the Graph of Cosine and Interpreting Graphs
1. Key Points for the Cosine Graph:
- The cosine graph is also a smooth wave, but it is shifted 90° to the left compared to the sine graph.
- It starts at its maximum (1) at 0°.
- The amplitude is the maximum height from the center line (which is 1 for both sin and cos).
- The period is the length of one complete cycle (360° for both).
- Interpreting these graphs builds analytical skills required for data analysis in Nigerian universities and industries.
diagramDescriptions: Graph of y = cos θ from 0° to 360° showing key points
Comparison of sine and cosine graphs
subUnits.youtubeQuery: SS1 Mathematics drawing graph of cosine interpreting trig graphs site:youtube.com

quiz.q: What is the value of cos 0°?
quiz.explanation: The cosine graph starts at (0°, 1).

quiz.q: At what angle does the cosine graph first cross the x-axis?
quiz.explanation: cos 90° = 0, so it crosses the x-axis at 90°.

quiz.q: What is the minimum value of the cosine graph?
quiz.explanation: The cosine wave reaches its lowest point at y = -1 at 180°.

quiz.q: How does the cosine graph compare to the sine graph?
opts: It is shifted 90° to the left
quiz.explanation: The cosine graph is the sine graph shifted 90° to the left (or right, depending on perspective).

quiz.q: What is the amplitude of the standard sine and cosine graphs?
quiz.explanation: The amplitude is the maximum distance from the center line, which is 1.

quiz.q: What is the period of the standard sine and cosine graphs?
quiz.explanation: One complete cycle of both graphs takes 360°.

quiz.q: At 180°, the cosine graph is at its:
quiz.explanation: cos 180° = -1, which is the minimum value.

quiz.q: At 360°, the cosine graph is at its:
quiz.explanation: cos 360° = 1, which is the maximum value, completing the cycle.

quiz.q: The cosine graph is positive in which quadrants?
quiz.explanation: Cosine is positive in Quadrant I (0-90) and Quadrant IV (270-360).

quiz.q: If a graph starts at (0,0) and goes up to (90,1), it is the graph of:
quiz.explanation: The sine graph starts at the origin and goes up to 1 at 90°.
